description: CreateConnector — Databricks clientCredentials OAuth M2M auth.
notes: UI auth-mode label: 'Client Credentials'. OAuth 2.0 machine-to-machine using a Databricks Service Principal's OAuth client_id + secret — distinct from 'Access Token' (PAT) mode.

authStrategy wire tag: `service_role` — same value as Access Token mode. The server distinguishes Access Token vs Client Credentials by which nested `databricksAuth.*` variant is populated (`pat` vs `clientCredentials`), NOT by `authStrategy`.
Client credentials live at `config.databricks.databricksAuth.clientCredentials.{clientId, clientSecret}` — two-level nesting mirrors Access Token's `databricksAuth.pat.token` shape.

Field diff vs Access Token mode: `databricksAuth.pat.{token}` is replaced by `databricksAuth.clientCredentials.{clientId, clientSecret}`. All other fields (name, host, httpPath, port, catalog, schema, connectorType, authStrategy) are identical. UI-required: name, host, httpPath, port, catalog, schema, clientId, clientSecret (8 fields; 1 more than Access Token because `clientId` + `clientSecret` replace a single `token`).
`clientId` is the Databricks Service Principal's applicationId (a UUID). `clientSecret` is the OAuth secret minted on the SP's Secrets tab. The SP must have CAN_USE on the target SQL warehouse.

Pre-create behavior: UI fires TestConnector first with the identical body; on 200 it fires CreateConnector. Unlike Snowflake's OAuth SSO mode, TestConnector works for Databricks Client Credentials — M2M is a complete credential and does not need a user handshake.

GetConnector persisted shape: `connector.databricksMetadata.{host, httpPath, port, databricksAuth, catalog, schema}`. `databricksAuth.clientCredentials.clientId` IS echoed (it is a non-sensitive UUID). `clientSecret` is absent (not empty-string) — only the secret half is hidden, matching PAT / password / privateKey behavior. `connector.authStrategy` = `service_role` at top level.
Wire name asymmetry: `databricks` request / `databricksMetadata` response (same as Access Token mode).
